factions.name: The Verrazzano Combine
factions.ideology: Everything that crosses water pays the Combine.
barks: The bridge sees everything, friend.
Tariffs went up. Condolences.
You walk dry, you owe the water something.
rumor: The Combine ran #cargo# through {neighborhood} on garbage barges last #weekday#.
A Tollman got promoted. His predecessor washed up under the pier, technically also promoted.

factions.name: Civic Shield Authority
factions.ideology: Order is a subscription service, and most of the city is behind on payments.
barks: Coverage check. Papers.
This block is current on its premiums. Move along.
Incidents are billable. Don't be an incident.
rumor: Shield pulled out of {neighborhood} overnight. Premiums lapsed, they said.
An Adjuster settled a dispute on #street# with a clipboard and two batons.

factions.name: The Remainder
factions.ideology: Someone has to actually do the job. Underfunded, unglamorous, occasionally heroic, perpetually furious.
barks: We still answer calls. Some calls.
File a report. I mean it. Paper still matters.
I knew this block when it had streetlights.
rumor: A Remainder sergeant solved the #street# killings on her own time. No one paid her for it.
The Remainder precinct heats one room and shares it with the evidence.

factions.name: The Wattmen
factions.ideology: Light is sold by the hour. Darkness is the alternative product.
barks: First of the month, friend.
Nice block. Be a shame if it browned out.
We keep the lights on. Remember who 'we' is.
rumor: The Wattmen cut #street# off at dinnertime over a missed payment. Surgical, block by block.
A Meter Man got caught skimming. They wired his bonus directly, so to speak.

factions.name: The Hollow Kings
factions.ideology: Every emptied building is a throne room. The Kings rule what everyone else abandoned.
barks: You're standing in the throne room.
Empty don't mean unclaimed.
Rent's free here. Leaving costs.
rumor: The Kings crowned a new Floorlord in the {neighborhood} towers. The coronation was audible for blocks.
Kings stripped #cargo# out of a sealed building in one night. Even the stairs.

factions.name: The Tenant League
factions.ideology: The lease is a treaty and the marshals broke it first.
barks: This block stands together.
Marshals came once. Once.
You current with your dues, neighbor?
rumor: The League turned back an eviction crew on #street# with sixty people and a drum line.
League street court fined a landlord his own boiler. Installed it themselves.

factions.name: Midtown Holdings Group
factions.ideology: The city is undervalued. Everything else is sentiment.
barks: This parcel is under management.
We bought the block. The block includes the sidewalk.
Relocation assistance is available. Briefly.
rumor: Holdings bought every deed on #street# through nine shell companies in one afternoon.
A Holdings VP toured {neighborhood} behind glass. The glass was rated for small arms.

factions.name: Harbor Wall Authority
factions.ideology: The wall holds because the Authority holds everything else.
barks: Dry feet aren't free.
The wall protects those who fund the wall.
Surge season's coming. Renew early.
rumor: The Authority condemned a dry block in {neighborhood} for 'wall purposes.' Condos by spring.
A Gate Captain opened the sluices on a rival's warehouse. Paperwork called it a drill.
